source_jobs.title: Senior Unity Developer (Anti Cheat)
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

Now we're looking for an Senior Unity Developer (Anti Cheat) with background in game development to take ownership of protecting Oxide from cheats and exploits.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Develop and evolve anti-cheat systems for Oxide: Survival Island.

 - Identify, analyze, and fix vulnerabilities in game code and gameplay logic.

 - Analyze logs, crashes, and runtime behavior to detect and investigate exploits.

 - Design solutions based on authoritative server principles.

 - Detect and counter common cheat patterns.

 - Research and improve anti-cheat detection methods and overall system robustness.

 - Integrate anti-cheat mechanisms into an existing codebase


WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - Hands-on experience building anti-cheat systems in multiplayer games (preferably shooters).

 - Experience working on multiplayer projects with authoritative server architecture.

 - Strong understanding of client-server interactions and networking in games.

 - Strong experience with Unity or other game engines.


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary.

 - Remote work option or relocation to Cyprus, with full company support including visa and housing assistance.

 - The chance to shape and influence an ambitious multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms.

 - A strong, supportive team passionate about creating great games.

 - A flexible work environment.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture that values creativity, initiative, and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Office Manager
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.


OFFICE MANAGER

We are looking for a responsible and organized Office Manager to ensure the smooth day-to-day operation of our office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Maintain the office environment and ensure everything is functioning properly

 - Keep the office clean, organized, and comfortable for employees and guests

 - Order, organize, and restock office supplies, groceries, and snacks

 - Coordinate with external contractors and service providers, including:
   
   - cleaning services
   
   - maintenance and repair teams
   
   - security services

 - Monitor office needs and proactively solve operational issues

 - Support a positive and efficient workplace atmosphere


REQUIREMENTS

 - Previous relevant experience in office administration or office management

 - Fluent English and Greek

 - Russian language is a strong advantage

 - Strong sense of responsibility and attention to detail

 - Love for organization, cleanliness, and order

 - Good communication and coordination skills

 - Proactive and reliable personality


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary.

 - Monthly lunch allowances via Wolt.

 - Health benefits - comprehensive medical insurance and SportBenefit membership.

 - Car rental.

 - School fee contribution for children.

 - A passionate and supportive team that values creativity and ownership.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture built on initiative, transparency, and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Senior QA Engineer
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

We are looking for Senior QA Engineers to join our team and contribute to the development and evolution of our flagship game, Oxide: Survival Island.

In this role, you will be responsible for ensuring the quality and stability of the game across all stages of development — from testing new features and gameplay mechanics to regression and release validation. If you enjoy working closely with developers, have a strong eye for detail, and are passionate about survival games, we’d love to hear from you.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Full-cycle testing of a mobile free-to-play project: testing new features and mechanics, regression testing, and acceptance testing.

 - Creating, maintaining, and updating test documentation (test cases, checklists).

 - Reporting and documenting found issues and preparing test reports.

 - Close collaboration with the development team.

 - Monitoring build stability and overall product quality.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 5+ years of experience in testing mobile games on Android and/or iOS, with a solid understanding of the full release cycle and QA processes.

 - Understanding of sandbox / survival game specifics: open world, crafting & loot systems, PvP/PvE mechanics.

 - Knowledge of client-server architecture and experience testing online/network gameplay.

 - Practical experience with QA tools and workflows:
   
   - bug tracking and test management systems (Jira, TestRail);
   
   - analytics platforms (Firebase, AppMetrica, GameAnalytics);
   
   - emulators (Bluestacks, Nox, etc.);
   
   - CI/CD systems (GitLab, TeamCity).

 - Experience working with development and debugging tools, including Android Studio, adb, logcat, Xcode, and Unity (basic navigation, assets, and component logic).

 - Familiarity with network traffic analysis tools (Charles, Fiddler, Wireshark) and version control systems (Git: pull, fetch, log).

 - Strong communication skills, ability to work in a multitasking environment, prioritize tasks effectively, and maintain a high level of attention to detail.

 - Experience working with AI tools (Claude, Gemini).

 - Knowledge of programming languages (C#, Python).

 - Experience in test automation for Unity-based mobile games.

 - English - B1+ level, Russian - C1 level.


BONUS IF YOU HAVE:

 - Passion for hardcore and survival games; hands-on experience with games like RUST, ARK, DayZ, or similar titles.

 - Experience working with integrations (Firebase).

 - Experience working with JSON files.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary.

 - Remote work format.

 - A passionate and supportive team that values creativity and ownership.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture built on initiative, transparency, and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Lead DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

We are looking for an experienced Lead SRE/DevOps Engineer to join our team and ensure the reliability, security, and scalability of our global infrastructure. You will play a key role in managing high-load environments, mitigating DDoS attacks, coordinating security updates, and maintaining centralized node management for distributed systems.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Team & Reliability Ownership: Own the DevOps/SRE function end-to-end — build and scale the team from scratch, define best practices, and establish processes to ensure 24/7 infrastructure reliability, including monitoring, on-call rotations, and incident response.

 - DDoS Mitigation: Design and implement strategies to prevent, detect, and mitigate DDoS attacks.

 - Incident Management: Handle severity updates and ensure rapid incident response and resolution.

 - Centralized Node Management: Deploy, configure, and manage a centralized node control system for efficient infrastructure governance.

 - Security Updates: Regularly apply security patches and updates to ensure system integrity and compliance.

 - Database Deployment: Set up and maintain databases in a highly available and fault-tolerant architecture.

 - Global Service Management: Manage and monitor servers across multiple regions, including US-based servers, ensuring consistent uptime and performance.

 - High-Load Infrastructure: Design and maintain scalable solutions to handle high traffic loads and ensure system resilience.

 - Pipeline Management: Ensure CI/CD pipelines run smoothly for reliable application deployment.

 - Security Best Practices: Implement and enforce security policies and procedures to protect infrastructure and data.

 - Node Lifecycle: Maintain, update, and scale nodes efficiently while minimizing downtime.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 5+ years of hands-on experience in DevOps / SRE roles, including work in high-load production environments.

 - Proven track record of building DevOps processes, infrastructure, and teams from scratch.

 - Hands-on experience with DDoS mitigation tools and practices.

 - Strong knowledge of centralized node management systems.

 - Solid understanding of cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or similar).

 - Proficiency with CI/CD pipelines and automation tools.

 - Experience with database deployment and management.

 - Solid grasp of security principles and experience applying security patches and updates.

 - Ability to manage distributed servers, including US-based environments.

 - Excellent problem-solving and incident response skills.

 - Strong communication and collaboration abilities.

 - English - B1+ level, Russian - C1 level.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Relocation support to Cyprus (Limassol), including visa and housing assistance (this is an on-site position).

 - Competitive salary.

 - Monthly lunch allowances via Wolt.

 - Health benefits - comprehensive medical insurance and SportBenefit membership.

 - Car rental.

 - Greek lessons.

 - A passionate and supportive team that values creativity and ownership.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture built on initiative, transparency, and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Senior 3D Animator
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

We are looking for a Senior 3D Animator to join the development of our flagship game, Oxide: Survival Island. You will be responsible for creating high-quality first-person and third-person animations for weapons, characters and in-game objects, while collaborating closely with designers, artists, and technical teams to bring immersive gameplay experiences to life.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Creating keyframe animations for characters, weapons, and gameplay objects in both first-person and third-person perspectives.

 - Cleaning and refining motion capture data to achieve polished in-game results.

 - Producing animations for skins and additional in-game content.

 - Preparing and exporting animation assets for integration into Unity.

 - Maintaining high animation quality with strong attention to posing, timing, and weight.


WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 5+ years of experience in animation production.

 - 3+ years of experience creating animations for games.

 - A strong portfolio showcasing different types of animation.

 - Experience working with both keyframe and mocap animation.

 - Experience animating first-person and third-person weapons.

 - Professional experience working with Maya.

 - Strong understanding of animation principles, including posing, timing, and sense of weight.

 - Understanding of how animation systems function inside game engines.

 - English - B1+ level, Russian - C1 level.


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary.

 - Flexible work options - you can work remotely.

 - The opportunity to contribute to an ambitious multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms.

 - A strong, supportive team passionate about creating great games.

 - A flexible and collaborative work environment.

 - Opportunities for professional and creative growth.

 - A culture that values creativity, ownership, and teamwork.

source_jobs.title: Senior Unity Developer
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

We are seeking an experienced Senior Unity Developer to join our team and contribute to the development and evolution of our flagship game, Oxide: Survival Island.

If you know Unity inside and out, including URP and rendering pipelines, you love digging into performance optimization, enjoy working with Addressables, shaders, and multiplayer systems, and eager to help build something meaningful from the ground up — we want to talk to you.

This role stays open on an ongoing basis because we’re always interested in meeting exceptional Unity Developers who can make a real impact on the project.




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Design, develop, and maintain core gameplay systems and features using Unity and C#.

 - Work with URP-based rendering, shaders, and visual effects with a strong focus on performance and visual quality on mobile devices.

 - Integrate Unity Addressables for scalable and dynamic UI content.

 - Develop and support multiplayer features.

 - Profile and optimize game performance: CPU/GPU usage, memory, loading times, and frame stability.

 - Support LiveOps and seasonal updates by delivering new features, systems, and technical improvements.

 - Collaborate closely with game designers, artists, and other engineers to turn game concepts into robust, scalable implementations.
   
   


WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 5+ years of experience in Unity development (ideally in mobile games).

 - Deep understanding of URP and Unity rendering workflows.

 - Strong C# skills and experience with performance optimization.

 - Experience designing and implementing game mechanics and UI systems.

 - Collaborative mindset and great communication skills.

 - Passion for games and crafting thoughtful user experiences.

 - Russian language proficiency at C1 level or higher.


BONUS IF YOU HAVE:

 - Shader experience (Shader Graph or HLSL).

 - Familiarity with Unity Addressables and runtime asset loading.

 - Experience with synchronous multiplayer systems.

 - Understanding of monetization flows and in-game economy.

 - Working in LiveOps or seasonal content environments.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary.

 - Relocation support to Cyprus, including visa and housing assistance.

 - Monthly lunch allowances via Wolt.

 - Health benefits - comprehensive medical insurance and SportBenefit membership.

 - Car rental.

 - Greek lessons.

 - School fee contribution for children.

 - A passionate and supportive team that values creativity and ownership.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture built on initiative, transparency, and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: 3D Artist (skins)
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

As a 3D Artist, you will specialize in creating high-quality, realistic textures and skin variations for weapons and in-game props. You will work closely with the design and development teams to create compelling, visually appealing 3D assets that enhance player immersion and drive gameplay mechanics.




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Create high-quality, realistic textures and skin variations for weapons and props, following the established visual style of Oxide.

 - Prepare and polish weapon and prop models for texturing, ensuring clean UVs and proper asset workflow.

 - Optimize textures and materials to ensure excellent performance on mobile devices.

 - Work closely with the art lead to maintain visual consistency across all skins and materials.

 - Iterate on feedback quickly and efficiently in a fast-paced production environment.

 - Collaborate with developers and technical artists to ensure smooth integration of assets into the game engine.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 3+ year of experience in 3D art.

 - Solid understanding of realistic texturing, including materials, roughness, dirt, and wear.

 - Proficiency with Substance Painter, Photoshop, and at least one 3D modeling package (Blender, Maya, or 3ds Max).

 - Basic knowledge of optimization techniques for mobile platforms.

 - Ability to follow art direction and adapt to an existing visual style.

 - Portfolio demonstrating prop and weapon texturing (skins, materials, or similar work).

 - English - B1+ level, Russian - C1 level.


BONUS IF YOU HAVE:

 - Familiarity with Unity or other mobile game engines.

 - Understanding of PBR workflows and mobile rendering constraints.

 - Interest in survival shooters or realistic art.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary.

 - Remote work format.

 - A passionate and supportive team that values creativity and ownership.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture built on initiative, transparency, and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Developer (C# / .NET)
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

We are seeking an experienced Senior Backend Developer to join our team and contribute to the development and evolution of our flagship game, Oxide: Survival Island.


WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING:

 - Build and operate multiple backend services in C# / .NET — game-facing APIs, player profiles, the in-game catalogue and economy, clans, chat, server orchestration, monetisation and player support. Most features cross service boundaries.

 - Own a slice of the analytics / data plane. Design schemas in an OLAP / columnar store, model ingestion pipelines that handle late-arriving updates and partial re-deliveries, build cohort / retention / ROAS / LTV aggregates, and surface them through a BI tool the LiveOps and marketing teams read every day.

 - Integrate monetisation and attribution. Receipt validation against the major mobile stores, reconciliation of payments from a third-party gateway, processing of attribution + cost data from a mobile-measurement provider. Catching missed callbacks, deduplicating noisy retries and gracefully recovering from refunds is where you'll spend more time than building the happy path.

 - Run real-time systems. A real-time pub-sub layer pushes chat, notifications and game-server events to clients; a key-value store backs sessions, last-seen indexes and leaderboards; cache invalidation across services is event-driven. You should be comfortable choosing the right pattern for each of these and tuning them under load.

 - Operate hundreds of dedicated game servers. A game-server panel exposes provisioning, draining, health and metrics. You'll write the backend logic that decides what runs where, surfaces incidents to operators and reroutes players when servers degrade.

 - Build the internal admin / LiveOps console. When LiveOps needs a new editor — for a config, an A/B test, a region setting, a chat filter, a remote popup — whoever built the backend side usually builds the UI side too. We don't keep a separate front-end team for this.

 - Run on Google Cloud. Services are containerised; data lives in managed stores; artefacts and ETL drops sit in object storage. You're expected to be at home there without being a Kubernetes specialist.

 - Pair with Unity engineers on the contract between client and server, and make sure the server stays the source of truth — most anti-cheat work is enforced on the backend.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 5+ years of backend experience, with at least one live, monetised product shipped end-to-end. Direct mobile-games experience is preferred but a strong ad-tech, fintech or marketplace background with the right instincts also fits.

 - Strong C# / .NET, including modern language features, async/await, performance intuition for allocations and HTTP client pooling, and comfort with dependency injection and the options pattern. (We use .NET 8 today, but the language version isn't the point.)

 - Experience with an OLAP / columnar database used in anger — ClickHouse, BigQuery, Druid, Pinot, or similar. You can design a schema that survives late-arriving data, reason about idempotent ETL, and read a query plan when something gets slow.

 - Experience with a document store and a key-value / pub-sub store in production — MongoDB, DynamoDB, Cosmos, etc. for the document side; Redis, KeyDB, Memcached and friends for caching and pub-sub. You should be able to articulate when to reach for which.

 - A working mental model of real-time and distributed systems — backpressure, idempotency, watermarks, eventual consistency, and the difference between a cache miss

source_jobs.title: 3D Environment & Prop Artist
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

As a 3D Environment & Prop Artist, you will be responsible for creating immersive, high-quality environments. You will work closely with the design and development teams to create believable open-world locations that support gameplay, exploration, and player immersion.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING:

 - Create high-quality 3D environment assets and props from concept to in-game implementation.

 - Develop both hero assets and modular asset kits for efficient world building.

 - Create high and low-poly models, UVs, textures, and materials optimized for mobile devices.

 - Build realistic locations, including terrain, vegetation, buildings, rocks, cliffs, and other world elements.

 - Collaborate with Level Designers to ensure environments support gameplay and visual goals.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 3+ years of experience as a 3D Environment Artist.

 - Strong understanding of the full environment art pipeline, from blockout and high/low-poly modeling to texturing, material setup, and Unity integration.

 - Experience creating environments for mobile games (preferably open-world projects).

 - Solid knowledge of mobile optimization techniques, including polygon budgets, texture memory management, atlases, trim sheets, LODs, batching, draw calls, overdraw, and readability on small screens.

 - Ability to create both high-quality hero assets and reusable modular environment and prop kits.

 - Understanding of modular workflows, asset reusability, visual consistency, and efficient level construction.

 - Strong grasp of environment composition, player navigation, silhouettes, spatial logic, and visual storytelling.

 - Experience creating terrain, vegetation, rocks, cliffs, buildings, props, and other key open-world environment elements.

 - Ability to bring environment areas to final in-game quality while balancing art direction, gameplay requirements, and performance budgets.

 - Portfolio showcasing realistic environment art and locations.

 - English - B1+ level, Russian - C1 level.


A STRONG PLUS:

 - Experience working with Unity URP, including materials, shaders, lighting, post-processing, and rendering limitations.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary.

 - Remote work format.

 - A passionate and supportive team that values creativity and ownership.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture built on initiative, transparency, and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Lead User Acquisition Manager
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

We are looking for a Lead User Acquisition Manager, who will take full ownership of the UA strategy and execution, including building and scaling UA processes, managing performance marketing channels, and driving sustainable growth.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Build and scale the User Acquisition process for our flagship game Oxide: Survival Island.

 - Plan, launch, and optimize user acquisition campaigns across multiple channels, taking full hands-on responsibility.

 - Own campaign analytics: track performance and work with key metrics.

 - Define requirements and briefs for ad creatives and evaluate their performance.

 - Continuously test new hypotheses, channels, and approaches to improve efficiency and scalability.

 - Prepare regular performance reports and insights for stakeholders.


WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - Solid UA experience in mobile products - mobile games or applications (3+ years).

 - Experience building UA processes from scratch.

 - Deep understanding and experience with the USA and LATAM markets.

 - Extensive experience with Google Ads, AppLovin, Meta, Unity Ads.

 - Strong analytical mindset and confidence working with data and predictions.

 - Ability to provide actionable feedback on ad creatives, including concepts, formats, and messaging.

 - Fluent English is a must; fluent Russian is a strong plus.


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Full ownership of UA strategy and execution.

 - Relocation support to Cyprus (Limassol), including visa and housing assistance (this is an on-site position).

 - Competitive salary.

source_jobs.title: Senior Game Designer
source_jobs.description:
HyperHug is an international game development studio specializing in the shooter genre. Our main office is based in Cyprus, but our talented team works remotely from around the world. We’re developing Oxide: Survival Island, an immersive multiplayer survival shooter for mobile platforms, and we’re always looking for motivated individuals who want to grow with us, contribute their creativity, and help shape the future of our games.

We are seeking a Game Designer to contribute to the development of our flagship game, Oxide: Survival Island. You will work across multiple layers of game design — from social features, onboarding, and progression to core gameplay improvements — while also supporting LiveOps and monetization initiatives.



We keep this role open on an ongoing basis because we’re always interested in meeting exceptional Game Designers who can make a real impact on the project.




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Design and develop meta systems that drive long-term engagement, including social features (e.g., clans), FTUE/onboarding, progression, and retention mechanics.

 - Contribute to core gameplay design, including reworking and improving core mechanics.

 - Plan and support LiveOps and monetization features, including events, passes, and offers, ensuring they are well integrated into the overall game ecosystem.

 - Collaborate with 2D/3D art teams: define requirements, brief leads, and manage asset pipelines for in-game features and content.

 - Configure and implement game content in Unity.

 - Run A/B tests, perform basic performance analysis, and prepare insights and recommendations for future iterations.




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

 - 3+ years of experience in Game Design with a focus on mid-core/hardcore multiplayer mobile games.

 - Strong experience designing complex game systems.

 - Experience running the full LiveOps loop: events, offers, testing, scheduling, performance analysis.

 - Successfully shipped monetization features with measurable impact on KPIs.

 - Proven work in cross-functional teams: dev, art, analytics, marketing.

 - Extensive player experience and strong interest in the shooter genre.

 - English - B1+ level, Russian - C1 level.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary.

 - Relocation support to Cyprus, including visa and housing assistance.

 - Monthly lunch allowances via Wolt.

 - Health benefits - comprehensive medical insurance and SportBenefit membership.

 - Car rental.

 - Greek lessons.

 - School fee contribution for children.

 - A passionate and supportive team that values creativity and ownership.

 - Opportunities for professional growth.

 - A culture built on initiative, transparency, and collaboration.

notes: Game development studio with a strong focus on engineering and design roles.
